ACM_CSS_taxonomy.name: Software
ACM_CSS_taxonomy.description: The study, design, development, testing, and maintenance of software systems and programs.

subcategories.name: GENERAL
subcategories.description: Encompasses general concepts and frameworks applicable across the software domain.

subcategories.name: PROGRAMMING TECHNIQUES
subcategories.description: The methods and methodologies used to write, structure, and organize source code.

subcategories.name: General
subcategories.description: Basic and non-specific programming techniques.

subcategories.name: Applicative (Functional) Programming
subcategories.description: Programming using functions as first-class entities (e.g., Lisp, Haskell).

subcategories.name: Automatic Programming
subcategories.description: Systems that enable computers to write or modify their own programs.

subcategories.name: Concurrent Programming
subcategories.description: Designing programs to execute multiple tasks simultaneously or in overlapping time periods.

subcategories.name: Sequential Programming
subcategories.description: Designing programs where instructions execute in a single, linear, step-by-step order.

subcategories.name: Object-oriented Programming
subcategories.description: A paradigm based on the concept of 'objects' to structure code and data.

subcategories.name: Logic Programming
subcategories.description: Programming based on formal logic (e.g., Prolog).

subcategories.name: Visual Programming
subcategories.description: Programming using graphical elements rather than textual code (e.g., drag-and-drop interfaces).

subcategories.name: Miscellaneous
subcategories.description: Other programming topics and techniques not specifically listed above.

subcategories.name: SOFTWARE ENGINEERING
subcategories.description: The systematic, disciplined, quantifiable approach to the development, operation, and maintenance of software.

subcategories.name: General
subcategories.description: Fundamental concepts and definitions in the field of software engineering.

subcategories.name: Requirements/Specifications
subcategories.description: The processes of identifying, documenting, and analyzing system needs and goals.

subcategories.name: Design Tools and Techniques
subcategories.description: The methods and aids used during the architectural design phase of a system.

subcategories.name: Coding Tools and Techniques
subcategories.description: The tools and practices supporting the implementation (coding) phase.

subcategories.name: Software/Program Verification
subcategories.description: Ensuring that the software product meets its specified requirements.

subcategories.name: Testing and Debugging
subcategories.description: Strategies for discovering and correcting errors and faults in code.

subcategories.name: Programming Environments
subcategories.description: Integrated toolsets and systems that support software development (IDEs).

subcategories.name: Distribution, Maintenance, and Enhancement
subcategories.description: Managing software delivery, bug fixes, updates, and future development after release.

subcategories.name: Metrics
subcategories.description: The measurement of software quality, product characteristics, and development processes.

subcategories.name: Management
subcategories.description: The administration of software projects, resources, and teams.

subcategories.name: Design
subcategories.description: Principles and practices for structuring a software solution.

subcategories.name: Software Architectures
subcategories.description: The fundamental structures and organization patterns of any software system.

subcategories.name: Interoperability
subcategories.description: The ability of different systems or components to work together and exchange information.

subcategories.name: Reusable Software
subcategories.description: Software components designed for re-application across various distinct applications.

subcategories.name: Miscellaneous
subcategories.description: Advanced or miscellaneous topics in software engineering.

subcategories.name: PROGRAMMING LANGUAGES
subcategories.description: The study of programming language design, theory, development, and execution mechanisms.

subcategories.name: General
subcategories.description: Concepts and issues applicable to all programming languages.

subcategories.name: Formal Definitions and Theory
subcategories.description: Mathematical and theoretical rules that describe language structure and meaning.

subcategories.name: Language Classifications
subcategories.description: Categorizing languages by their underlying paradigm (e.g., procedural, functional, object-oriented).

subcategories.name: Language Constructs and Features
subcategories.description: The structural elements and functionalities available within a language (e.g., inheritance, loops).

subcategories.name: Processors
subcategories.description: Programs that process source code (e.g., compilers, interpreters, assemblers).

subcategories.name: Miscellaneous
subcategories.description: Other topics related to programming languages.

subcategories.name: OPERATING SYSTEMS
subcategories.description: The systems responsible for managing and controlling computer hardware resources and providing a platform for applications.

subcategories.name: General
subcategories.description: The overarching concepts and goals of operating systems.

subcategories.name: Process Management
subcategories.description: The handling of tasks, processes, synchronization, and processor scheduling.

subcategories.name: Storage Management
subcategories.description: The administration of primary memory, virtual memory, and caching.

subcategories.name: File Systems Management
subcategories.description: Organizing, storing, and retrieving data on permanent storage media.

subcategories.name: Communications Management
subcategories.description: Controlling communication and information exchange between processes or systems.

subcategories.name: Reliability
subcategories.description: Ensuring the system's ability to operate without failure for specified time periods.

subcategories.name: Security and Protection
subcategories.description: Mechanisms for access control and safeguarding resources and data.

subcategories.name: Organization and Design
subcategories.description: The architectural structure and internal design of operating systems.

subcategories.name: Performance
subcategories.description: Measuring and optimizing the efficiency and speed of the operating system.

subcategories.name: Systems Programs and Utilities
subcategories.description: Auxiliary programs providing system administration services (e.g., shells, file managers).

subcategories.name: Miscellaneous
subcategories.description: Other diverse topics in operating systems.

subcategories.name: MISCELLANEOUS
subcategories.description: Areas or miscellaneous topics not fully covered in the main Software subcategories.

ACM_CSS_taxonomy.name: Data
ACM_CSS_taxonomy.description: Classification concerning the organization, encryption, storage, and retrieval of information.

subcategories.name: GENERAL
subcategories.description: General concepts and topics related to data.

subcategories.name: DATA STRUCTURES
subcategories.description: Specific ways of organizing data in a computer (e.g., arrays, lists, trees).

subcategories.name: DATA STORAGE REPRESENTATIONS
subcategories.description: How data is physically represented on storage media (e.g., sequential or direct access).

subcategories.name: DATA ENCRYPTION
subcategories.description: The transformation of data into a secret code for security purposes (cryptography).

subcategories.name: CODING AND INFORMATION THEORY
subcategories.description: Mathematical principles for measuring the efficiency, capacity, and security of data transmission and storage.

subcategories.name: FILES
subcategories.description: The organization and management of collections of persistently stored data.

subcategories.name: MISCELLANEOUS
subcategories.description: Other diverse topics in the data domain.

ACM_CSS_taxonomy.name: Theory of Computation
ACM_CSS_taxonomy.description: The study of the limits, capabilities, and efficiency of computation (what can be computed and how).

subcategories.name: GENERAL
subcategories.description: General concepts and goals of the theory of computation.

subcategories.name: COMPUTATION BY ABSTRACT DEVICES
subcategories.description: Abstract models of computation that define the mechanisms of calculation (e.g., Turing Machines).

subcategories.name: General
subcategories.description: General topics for abstract models of computation.

subcategories.name: Models of Computation
subcategories.description: Theoretical systems representing the calculation process (e.g., finite automata).

subcategories.name: Modes of Computation
subcategories.description: Methods of conducting computation (e.g., parallel, sequential, randomized).

subcategories.name: Complexity Measures and Classes
subcategories.description: Categorizing problems by the resources required for their solution (e.g., P and NP).

subcategories.name: Miscellaneous
subcategories.description: Other topics within computation by abstract devices.

subcategories.name: ANALYSIS OF ALGORITHMS AND PROBLEM COMPLEXITY
subcategories.description: Analyzing the efficiency of algorithms in terms of time and space required to solve problems.

subcategories.name: General
subcategories.description: General concepts of algorithm analysis.

subcategories.name: Numerical Algorithms and Problems
subcategories.description: Analysis of algorithms related to digital mathematical operations (e.g., solving equations).

subcategories.name: Nonnumerical Algorithms and Problems
subcategories.description: Analysis of non-digital algorithms (e.g., sorting, searching, graph traversal).

subcategories.name: Tradeoffs between Complexity Measures
subcategories.description: The compromises between different complexity metrics (e.g., space vs. time).

subcategories.name: Miscellaneous
subcategories.description: Other topics within the analysis of algorithms and problem complexity.

subcategories.name: LOGICS AND MEANINGS OF PROGRAMS
subcategories.description: The use of formal logic to describe and verify the correctness and integrity of programs.

subcategories.name: General
subcategories.description: Fundamental concepts of logic in the programming context.

subcategories.name: Specifying and Verifying and Reasoning about Programs
subcategories.description: Formal methodologies for proving program correctness and behavior.

subcategories.name: Semantics of Programming Languages
subcategories.description: The study of the precise meaning of programming language statements (formal and informal).

subcategories.name: Studies of Program Constructs
subcategories.description: Analysis of the structural elements of programs from a logical standpoint.

subcategories.name: Miscellaneous
subcategories.description: Other topics within logics and meanings of programs.

subcategories.name: MATHEMATICAL LOGIC AND FORMAL LANGUAGES
subcategories.description: The mathematical foundations that study valid inference and languages processable by machines.

subcategories.name: General
subcategories.description: Basic concepts in mathematical logic and formal languages.

subcategories.name: Mathematical Logic
subcategories.description: The study of logic as a mathematical tool (e.g., first-order logic).

subcategories.name: Grammars and Other Rewriting Systems
subcategories.description: Formal systems that describe language structure (e.g., Chomsky grammars).

subcategories.name: Formal Languages
subcategories.description: Languages defined by precise syntactic rules (e.g., regular languages, context-free languages).

subcategories.name: Miscellaneous
subcategories.description: Other diverse topics.

subcategories.name: MISCELLANEOUS
subcategories.description: Other diverse topics in the theory of computation.

ACM_CSS_taxonomy.name: Mathematics of Computing
ACM_CSS_taxonomy.description: Mathematical disciplines considered fundamental to the concepts and applications of computer science.

subcategories.name: GENERAL
subcategories.description: General concepts and goals of the mathematics of computing.

subcategories.name: NUMERICAL ANALYSIS
subcategories.description: The study of algorithms that use numerical approximation to solve continuous mathematical problems.

subcategories.name: General
subcategories.description: Fundamental concepts of numerical analysis.

subcategories.name: Interpolation
subcategories.description: Estimating unknown points within the range of known data points.

subcategories.name: Approximation
subcategories.description: Using simpler functions to approximate functions.

subcategories.name: Numerical Linear Algebra
subcategories.description: Numerically solving linear problems (e.g., systems of linear equations, eigenvalues).

subcategories.name: Quadrature and Numerical Differentiation
subcategories.description: Numerically calculating integrals and derivatives.

subcategories.name: Roots of Nonlinear Equations
subcategories.description: Finding solutions to nonlinear equations numerically.

subcategories.name: Optimization
subcategories.description: Techniques for finding the best possible solution to a problem (maxima and minima).

subcategories.name: Ordinary Differential Equations
subcategories.description: Numerically solving ordinary differential equations.

subcategories.name: Partial Differential Equations
subcategories.description: Numerically solving partial differential equations.

subcategories.name: Integral Equations
subcategories.description: Solving equations containing an unknown function under an integral sign.

subcategories.name: Applications
subcategories.description: Real-world uses of numerical analysis techniques.

subcategories.name: Miscellaneous
subcategories.description: Other topics within numerical analysis.

subcategories.name: DISCRETE MATHEMATICS
subcategories.description: The study of countable, distinctly separate mathematical structures, fundamental to algorithms and data structures.

subcategories.name: General
subcategories.description: Basic concepts of discrete mathematics.

subcategories.name: Combinatorics
subcategories.description: The study of finite, discrete structures and counting.

subcategories.name: Graph Theory
subcategories.description: The study of graphs, which are mathematical structures used to model pairwise relations between objects.

subcategories.name: Applications
subcategories.description: Real-world uses of discrete mathematics.

subcategories.name: Miscellaneous
subcategories.description: Other topics within discrete mathematics.

subcategories.name: PROBABILITY AND STATISTICS
subcategories.description: The mathematical modeling of uncertainty and the analysis of data to draw inferences.

subcategories.name: MATHEMATICAL SOFTWARE
subcategories.description: Software tools and packages designed for mathematical computation and modeling.

subcategories.name: MISCELLANEOUS
subcategories.description: Other topics in the mathematics of computing.

ACM_CSS_taxonomy.name: Information Systems
ACM_CSS_taxonomy.description: The study of systems for collecting, storing, retrieving, processing, and disseminating information.

subcategories.name: GENERAL
subcategories.description: General concepts and topics related to information systems.

subcategories.name: MODELS AND PRINCIPLES
subcategories.description: The theoretical frameworks and foundational concepts underlying information systems.

subcategories.name: General
subcategories.description: General concepts of information system models and principles.

subcategories.name: Systems and Information Theory
subcategories.description: The application of theoretical concepts of information and systems to IS design.

subcategories.name: User/Machine Systems
subcategories.description: The study of interaction between users and information systems.

subcategories.name: Miscellaneous
subcategories.description: Other topics within models and principles.

subcategories.name: DATABASE MANAGEMENT
subcategories.description: The design, implementation, and maintenance of systems for storing and retrieving large volumes of structured data.

subcategories.name: General
subcategories.description: Fundamental concepts and definitions in database management.

subcategories.name: Logical Design
subcategories.description: The abstract structure of the database (e.g., relational, object-oriented models).

subcategories.name: Physical Design
subcategories.description: How the database is physically stored on disk and accessed efficiently.

subcategories.name: Languages
subcategories.description: Languages used to interact with the database (e.g., SQL, DDL, DML).

subcategories.name: Systems
subcategories.description: The architecture and functionality of Database Management Systems (DBMSs).

subcategories.name: Heterogeneous Databases
subcategories.description: Systems that integrate multiple distinct and independent databases.

subcategories.name: Database Machines
subcategories.description: Hardware systems optimized specifically for database operations.

subcategories.name: Database Administration
subcategories.description: The operational tasks of managing, securing, and maintaining a database system.

subcategories.name: Database Applications
subcategories.description: Practical uses and systems built on top of database technology.

subcategories.name: Miscellaneous
subcategories.description: Other topics in database management.

subcategories.name: INFORMATION STORAGE AND RETRIEVAL
subcategories.description: The techniques and methods for indexing, searching, and accessing information, especially text and documents.

subcategories.name: General
subcategories.description: Core concepts of information storage and retrieval (ISR).

subcategories.name: Content Analysis and Indexing
subcategories.description: Analyzing content to extract terms and create indexes for efficient searching.

subcategories.name: Information Storage
subcategories.description: Methods and architectures for the organization and permanent saving of information.

subcategories.name: Information Search and Retrieval
subcategories.description: Algorithms and strategies for querying and retrieving relevant items from a store.

subcategories.name: Systems and Software
subcategories.description: The platforms and programs that implement ISR functionalities.

subcategories.name: Online Information Services
subcategories.description: Web-based and remote services for accessing information.

subcategories.name: Library Automation
subcategories.description: Using computer systems to manage library operations.

subcategories.name: Digital Libraries
subcategories.description: Organized collections of digital content accessible online.

subcategories.name: Miscellaneous
subcategories.description: Other topics in information storage and retrieval.

subcategories.name: INFORMATION SYSTEMS APPLICATIONS
subcategories.description: The use of information systems principles to solve real-world problems in various organizational settings.

subcategories.name: General
subcategories.description: General applications of information systems.

subcategories.name: Office Automation
subcategories.description: Using technology to streamline and automate routine office tasks.

subcategories.name: Types of Systems
subcategories.description: Classification of systems by function (e.g., TPS, MIS, EIS).

subcategories.name: Communications Applications
subcategories.description: IS used for internal and external communication (e.g., email, groupware).

subcategories.name: Miscellaneous
subcategories.description: Other applications of information systems.

subcategories.name: INFORMATION INTERFACES AND PRESENTATION (e.g., HCI)
subcategories.description: The design, evaluation, and study of the interfaces through which users interact with information systems (now often called HCI).

subcategories.name: General
subcategories.description: Core concepts of information interfaces and presentation.

subcategories.name: Multimedia Information Systems
subcategories.description: Systems that handle and present information using multiple media formats (audio, video, text).

subcategories.name: User Interfaces
subcategories.description: The design and evaluation of the points of interaction between humans and computers.

subcategories.name: Group and Organization Interfaces
subcategories.description: Interfaces supporting collaborative work and organizational communication (Groupware).

subcategories.name: Hypertext/Hypermedia
subcategories.description: Non-linear organization of information links (e.g., the World Wide Web).

subcategories.name: Sound and Music Computing
subcategories.description: Processing, analysis, and generation of audio and musical data.

subcategories.name: Miscellaneous
subcategories.description: Other topics in interfaces and presentation.

subcategories.name: MISCELLANEOUS
subcategories.description: Other topics in information systems.

ACM_CSS_taxonomy.name: Computing Methodologies
ACM_CSS_taxonomy.description: The methods and techniques used to solve complex computational problems (often involving non-numerical symbolic processing).

subcategories.name: GENERAL
subcategories.description: General concepts and topics related to computing methodologies.

subcategories.name: SYMBOLIC AND ALGEBRAIC MANIPULATION
subcategories.description: Systems and techniques for manipulating mathematical expressions and symbols rather than numerical values.

subcategories.name: General
subcategories.description: Core concepts of symbolic and algebraic manipulation.

subcategories.name: Expressions and Their Representation
subcategories.description: How mathematical formulas and terms are structured and stored.

subcategories.name: Algorithms
subcategories.description: Algorithms for operations like simplification, differentiation, and integration of symbolic expressions.

subcategories.name: Languages and Systems
subcategories.description: Programming languages and software dedicated to symbolic manipulation (e.g., Mathematica, Maple).

subcategories.name: Applications
subcategories.description: Real-world uses of symbolic and algebraic manipulation.

subcategories.name: Miscellaneous
subcategories.description: Other topics in symbolic manipulation.

subcategories.name: ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE
subcategories.description: The theory and development of computer systems able to perform tasks normally requiring human intelligence.

subcategories.name: General
subcategories.description: Fundamental concepts and goals of artificial intelligence (AI).

subcategories.name: Applications and Expert Systems
subcategories.description: AI systems designed to solve real-world problems or mimic human experts.

subcategories.name: Automatic Programming
subcategories.description: AI techniques used to generate or modify programs.

subcategories.name: Deduction and Theorem Proving
subcategories.description: Systems that derive logical conclusions from given premises.

subcategories.name: Knowledge Representation Formalisms and Methods
subcategories.description: The structure and rules used to encode information for use by an AI system.

subcategories.name: Programming Languages and Software
subcategories.description: Languages and tools optimized for AI development (e.g., Lisp, Prolog).

subcategories.name: Learning
subcategories.description: Algorithms enabling systems to improve performance based on data or experience (Machine Learning).

subcategories.name: Natural Language Processing
subcategories.description: Systems for understanding, interpreting, and generating human language.

subcategories.name: Problem Solving, Control Methods, and Search
subcategories.description: Algorithms for finding solutions in a search space (e.g., heuristic search, planning).

subcategories.name: Robotics
subcategories.description: The design and control of intelligent, programmable machines.

subcategories.name: Vision and Scene Understanding
subcategories.description: Interpreting visual data to reconstruct a model of the physical world (Computer Vision).

subcategories.name: Distributed Artificial Intelligence
subcategories.description: Cooperative intelligent agents and multi-agent systems.

subcategories.name: Miscellaneous
subcategories.description: Other diverse topics in artificial intelligence.

subcategories.name: COMPUTER GRAPHICS
subcategories.description: The creation, manipulation, and display of visual content using computers.

subcategories.name: General
subcategories.description: Fundamental concepts and definitions in computer graphics.

subcategories.name: Hardware Architecture
subcategories.description: Specialized hardware components for graphics processing (e.g., GPUs, display systems).

subcategories.name: Graphics Systems
subcategories.description: Software and hardware configurations for rendering and display.

subcategories.name: Picture/Image Generation
subcategories.description: Techniques for creating images (e.g., rasterization, ray tracing).

subcategories.name: Graphics Utilities
subcategories.description: Software tools and functions for graphics manipulation.

subcategories.name: Computational Geometry and Object Modeling
subcategories.description: Mathematical and algorithmic methods for representing and manipulating 2D/3D shapes.

subcategories.name: Methodology and Techniques
subcategories.description: General methods for rendering and visual representation.

subcategories.name: Three-Dimensional Graphics and Realism
subcategories.description: Creating realistic or non-realistic 3D representations and animation.

subcategories.name: Applications
subcategories.description: Real-world uses of computer graphics (e.g., CAD, visualization).

subcategories.name: Miscellaneous
subcategories.description: Other topics in computer graphics.

subcategories.name: IMAGE PROCESSING AND COMPUTER VISION
subcategories.description: Techniques for manipulating digital images and deriving high-level understanding from visual data.

subcategories.name: General
subcategories.description: Core concepts of image processing and computer vision.

subcategories.name: Digitization and Image Capture
subcategories.description: Converting physical images into digital format.

subcategories.name: Compression (Coding)
subcategories.description: Algorithms for reducing the size of image data (e.g., JPEG, MPEG).

subcategories.name: Enhancement
subcategories.description: Improving the visual quality of an image (e.g., contrast, sharpness).

subcategories.name: Restoration
subcategories.description: Recovering a degraded image to its original state (e.g., noise removal).

subcategories.name: Reconstruction
subcategories.description: Creating an image from non-image data (e.g., medical scans).

subcategories.name: Segmentation
subcategories.description: Dividing an image into meaningful regions or objects.

subcategories.name: Feature Measurement
subcategories.description: Quantifying characteristics of objects in an image (e.g., size, shape).

subcategories.name: Scene Analysis
subcategories.description: Interpreting the content and structure of a visual scene.

subcategories.name: Applications
subcategories.description: Practical uses of image processing (e.g., remote sensing, medical imaging).

subcategories.name: Image Representation
subcategories.description: How images are formally structured and stored in a computer.

subcategories.name: Miscellaneous
subcategories.description: Other topics in image processing and computer vision.

subcategories.name: PATTERN RECOGNITION
subcategories.description: The automatic discovery of regularities in data using computer algorithms, and the use of these regularities to classify data.

subcategories.name: General
subcategories.description: Fundamental concepts of pattern recognition.

subcategories.name: Models
subcategories.description: Mathematical models used for pattern classification (e.g., statistical, syntactic).

subcategories.name: Design Methodology
subcategories.description: Systematic approaches for building pattern recognition systems.

subcategories.name: Clustering
subcategories.description: Grouping a set of objects such that objects in the same group are more similar to each other than to those in other groups.

subcategories.name: Applications
subcategories.description: Practical uses of pattern recognition (e.g., voice recognition, biometrics).

subcategories.name: Implementation
subcategories.description: The realization of pattern recognition algorithms in hardware or software.

subcategories.name: Miscellaneous
subcategories.description: Other topics in pattern recognition.

subcategories.name: SIMULATION AND MODELING
subcategories.description: The development and use of abstract models to imitate the behavior of real-world systems over time.

subcategories.name: General
subcategories.description: Core concepts of simulation and modeling.

subcategories.name: Simulation Theory
subcategories.description: Mathematical and logical principles underlying simulation.

subcategories.name: Simulation Languages
subcategories.description: Specialized languages for building simulation models (e.g., SIMULA).

subcategories.name: Applications
subcategories.description: Real-world uses of simulation (e.g., traffic flow, climate modeling).

subcategories.name: Model Validation and Analysis
subcategories.description: Checking if the model accurately represents the real system.

subcategories.name: Model Development
subcategories.description: The process of creating and refining a simulation model.

subcategories.name: Simulation Output Analysis
subcategories.description: Interpreting and deriving conclusions from simulation results.

subcategories.name: Simulation Support Systems
subcategories.description: Software tools that aid in the development and execution of simulations.

subcategories.name: Types of Simulation
subcategories.description: Categorization by methodology (e.g., discrete event, continuous, Monte Carlo).

subcategories.name: Miscellaneous
subcategories.description: Other topics in simulation and modeling.

subcategories.name: DOCUMENT AND TEXT PROCESSING
subcategories.description: The creation, editing, formatting, storage, and retrieval of documents and large volumes of text.

subcategories.name: General
subcategories.description: Core concepts of document and text processing.

subcategories.name: Document and Text Editing
subcategories.description: Tools and techniques for modifying textual content.

subcategories.name: Document Preparation
subcategories.description: Formatting and structuring documents for output (e.g., word processors, LaTeX).

subcategories.name: Index Generation
subcategories.description: Automated creation of indexes for fast retrieval.

subcategories.name: Electronic Publishing
subcategories.description: Using electronic means to prepare and distribute publications.

subcategories.name: Document Capture
subcategories.description: Converting paper documents into digital formats (e.g., scanning, OCR).

subcategories.name: Miscellaneous
subcategories.description: Other topics in document and text processing.

subcategories.name: MISCELLANEOUS
subcategories.description: Other diverse topics in computing methodologies.

ACM_CSS_taxonomy.name: Computer Applications
ACM_CSS_taxonomy.description: The practical use of computer systems and software across various non-computer science domains and industries.

subcategories.name: GENERAL
subcategories.description: General concepts of computer applications.

subcategories.name: ADMINISTRATIVE DATA PROCESSING
subcategories.description: Computer use in business, finance, and record-keeping (e.g., payroll, accounting).

subcategories.name: PHYSICAL SCIENCES AND ENGINEERING
subcategories.description: Computer applications in fields like physics, chemistry, geology, and civil/electrical engineering.

subcategories.name: LIFE AND MEDICAL SCIENCES
subcategories.description: Computer use in biology, bioinformatics, medicine, and healthcare (e.g., imaging, modeling).

subcategories.name: SOCIAL AND BEHAVIORAL SCIENCES
subcategories.description: Applications in fields like psychology, sociology, economics, and political science.

subcategories.name: ARTS AND HUMANITIES
subcategories.description: Computer use in music, visual arts, literature, and history.

subcategories.name: COMPUTER-AIDED ENGINEERING
subcategories.description: Systems supporting engineering design and manufacturing (e.g., CAD/CAM).

subcategories.name: COMPUTERS IN OTHER SYSTEMS
subcategories.description: Embedded and dedicated computer systems within larger machines (e.g., control systems, industrial automation).

subcategories.name: MISCELLANEOUS
subcategories.description: Other diverse topics in computer applications.
